qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Host-scoped token can read and modify arbitrary hosts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about `GET /api/extclients/{network}` and `GET /api/nodes/{network}` leaking WireGuard private keys and missing per-user filtering, while this finding targets missing host-ID binding on host-token routes such as `/api/v1/fallback/host/{hostid}` and `/api/v1/host/{hostid}/peer_info`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable list handlers or the private-key disclosure root cause.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes a `platform-user` obtaining WireGuard private keys by calling `GET /api/extclients/{network}` or `GET /api/nodes/{network}` because those list endpoints return full records without ownership filtering.

The public fix commit `5617d97c` (`NM-258: hide extclient private keys in list api`) changes `controllers/ext_client.go` to filter `GET /api/extclients/{network}` results by user access and blank `PrivateKey`, and changes `controllers/node.go` to blank `StaticNode.PrivateKey` in node list responses.

The only qualifying finding instead reports that any valid host token can target another host ID on `/api/v1/fallback/host/{hostid}` and `/api/v1/host/{hostid}/peer_info` because the authenticated host identity is not bound to the path parameter.
